gleyber torres: Torres is a reliable source of power at second base and is coming off a solid All-Star season in Detroit where he accepted a qualifying offer. He underwent sports hernia surgery in October but is expected to be fully ready for the season, providing a stable, if not sky-high ceiling, floor for HR and RBI.

Diaz provides power from the catcher position, hitting 20 home runs in 2025. However, his aggressive, free-swinging approach led to a disappointing .256 average last season and makes him a volatile asset despite the power potential.
marcus semien: Now with the Mets, Semien is projected to hit in a much-improved lineup, which should provide ample run and RBI opportunities. At age 35, there's age-related decline risk, but he's a durable player who offers a solid floor across four categories and is priced as a mid-round value.
jordan westburg: Westburg offers a tantalizing power/speed blend, but a recent UCL tear has him out through April, making him a high-risk pick. His ADP is dropping, but waiting on this injury in a deep league is a dangerous game when you need a starter now.
gabriel moreno: Moreno is an elite defensive catcher whose bat showed significant promise in 2025 with a .285 average, though injuries limited him to 83 games. His high-contact approach provides a safe floor for batting average, and underlying metrics suggest a power surge could be coming if he stays healthy.
alejandro kirk: Kirk is a high-floor catcher who excels in batting average and on-base percentage due to his elite plate discipline. While he won't provide elite power, his guaranteed playing time and ability to stabilize your ratios make him a valuable and safe pick, especially if you've already drafted power hitters.
